mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and retrieval of unstructured data, such as images and text. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to fully managed solutions.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Milvus using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Milvus Helm repository.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install Milvus CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Milvus to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

```

> Installs the MilvusCluster CRD.
> Ensure CRDs are installed before proceeding.

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Milvus in the specified namespace using Helm, allowing you to customize the installation parameters.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

> Installs Milvus version 2.5.0 in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Milvus cluster by applying a custom resource definition (CRD) that defines the cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

```

> Creates a Milvus cluster with the specified configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Milvus installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Milvus pods to ensure that the installation was successful and that the cluster is running.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Lists all pods in the 'milvus' namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the Milvus cluster.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstalls the Milvus release from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade your Milvus installation, including backing up data and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.6.0

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Drains all pods in the 'milvus' namespace before upgrade.
> Upgrades Milvus to version 2.6.0.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n milvus -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MilvusCluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MilvusCluster CRD may not be installed or applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus pod may be crashing due to resource constraints or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus and adjust resource requests/limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be exposed correctly or is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running with kubectl get svc -n milvus and check the service configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the Milvus pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your Kubernetes nodes or adjust the resource requests in the Milvus configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old indexing methods in favor of the new algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to migrate your data using the provided migration tools.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all Milvus pods in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n milvus'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
